SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on most aspects.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells.  Winds 20 -30 mph.
Comment: If accessing main Cairngorms from the South, worth consulting North Cairngorms reports.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will rise above the summits throughout. Rain overnight but dry during the day. A strong breeze throughout, Southerly overnight, South-South-Westerly during the day.
SnowStability: High freezing levels with rain will lead to an unstable period overnight but by dawn the majority of the snow will be soft, wet but generally well bonded. However wet snow  instabilities may persist on the steepest terrain mostly on North-West through North-East to East aspects above 900 metres.  Remaining cornices and icefall will be prone to collapse.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A cloudy but dry day. The freezing level did rise throughout the day, around -1 degrees early afternoon. Strong winds from South-South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: Localised unstable windslab persists in steeper wind sheltered locations on North-West through North to East aspects above 950 metres. Worst affected locations are gully tops and steep slopes around corrie rims. Elsewhere old snow is firm and icy particularly at higher locations. Stability of remaining cornices is suspect.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Very hard snow ice on exposed windward slopes. At lower elevations a softening of the snowpack during the day, especially where exposed to any sunlight. Cloud base lingered around 1000 metres with the occasional break.  Moderate summit winds.
Comment: At high elevations, little change expected in the snowpack with many areas remaining firm and icy.  Negligible amounts  of fresh snowfall expected during the afternoon.
WeatherInfluences: Very strong Southerly winds will ease slightly around dawn veering South-South-West. Mainly dry with some negligible drizzle overnight and a trace of snow in the afternoon. The freezing level will lower to 1200 metres by the morning.
SnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab will persist on steep wind sheltered North and North-East aspects above 1000 metres. Localised instabilities will be found on other aspects. Gully tops, steep convexities and coire rims will be most affected. Cornices will remain a threat from collapse on West through North to East aspects. Wind exposed areas will be very firm, icy and stable.  At lower elevations the snowpack will continue to consolidate. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry day with moderate summit winds from the South-West. The freezing level was around 1100 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab persist on North and North-East aspects above 1000 metres. Localised instabilities can be found in other aspects. Steep wind sheltered locations are most affected such as convexities, gully tops and coire rims. Cornices remain a hazard. Wind exposed areas are icy, firm and have good stability.  A gradual softening of the snowpack through the day at lower elevations and where exposed to the sun. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Diminished snowpack, good above 800 metres very firm.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells.  Cool and light winds  on the tops
Comment: A mild day but any lying snow remains firm and hard  with potential for very consequential slips and trips.
WeatherInfluences: Winds will be strong  Southerly overnight veering South-Westerly during the day remaining strong. Freezing level around summits. Mostly dry with precipitation falling as sleet and snow showers during the afternoon at summit levels.
SnowStability: The snowpack that is present on mostly North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 800m and mainly in gullies, coire back walls and extensive fields in some places will be well bonded and stable throughout. Patchy below this level. Any new accumulations of summit snow are not expected to be significant.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: An overcast and cool day with a freezing level around summit levels. Dry
ObservedSnowStability: Little change, the old snowpack that is present on mostly North-West through North to Easterly aspects in mainly gullies and coire back walls and sometimes extensive fields above 800m is well bonded and stable throughout. Although mild, even the patches below this level are firm and stable. The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Generally firm snowpack.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts
Comment: Snowpack remaining firm, particularly on higher summits.
WeatherInfluences: Although a light rain or sleet shower can\'t be ruled out it will be generally dry. The freezing level will be around the summits creeping downward during the day.
SnowStability: Little change is expected. Above about 1100 metres the snowpack will be firm and icy, below this level it will be softer. In both cases stability will be good.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry day with the freezing level rising to around 1200 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: In the morning the snowpack was firm in all locations. It softened slightly through the day below about 1000 metres.  Stability remains generally good throughout.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Wet snow throughout, still quite firm in most places. Good visibility. Winds around 20 mph.
WeatherInfluences: A strong Southerly wind eases and veers to the South-West in the afternoon. Showers will mostly fall as rain with sleet or wet snow only on the higher summits. The freezing level will drop from above the summits to around 1000 metres by the end of the period.
SnowStability: The patchy snowpack will remain well bonded and stable in all locations. Fresh snow accumulations on the higher summits are not expected to be significant. Greatest snow cover will be on North-West through North to East aspects above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry morning with moderate Southerly winds. Some rain reached the West of the area in the afternoon. The freezing level was above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The patchy snowpack has good stability in all locations. Greatest snow cover is on North-West through North to East aspects above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice with surface softening.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Winds approx 10mph to 15mph.
Comment:
Icy patches remain with potential for very consequential slips and trips.

Showery on Tuesday with wintery conditions on the summits.
WeatherInfluences: Mainly dry overnight and through the morning. A period of patchy rain around the middle of the day only falling as sleet on the highest summits. Southerly winds will be fresh to strong.
SnowStability: The remaining snowpack will continue to slowly thaw and diminish at all elevations becoming increasingly soft and wet. Stability will be generally good. Largest continuous snow areas will be on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Dry and overcast with cloudy summits and some brighter intervals. Moderate South-Easterly winds with the freezing level around the highest summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The generally firm and stable snowpack is beginning to thaw at all elevations resulting in a thin surface layer of soft wet snow. Largest continuous snow areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.
